pattern: Reservoir sampling generalizes past 'the' index: scanning once and swapping the held answer for the k-th matching index with probability 1/k picks uniformly among every occurrence of a repeated target, all in O(1) memory.
hints: Precomputing every index for every distinct value in a hash map at construction time makes each pick O(1) but costs O(n) memory up front.
Without extra memory, scan the array on each pick and count how many times the target has been seen so far.
Keep only the most recently seen matching index, replacing it with probability 1/(matches seen so far) — the count resets on every call to pick since the array itself never changes.

edge_inputs.description: target with several occurrences, repeated picks
target occurs exactly once, at index 0 — the answer is fully determined
every element equals the target — any index is legal
target occurs at the last index only — the answer is fully determined
negative values with a repeated target
multiple distinct targets queried in sequence against the same array
extreme int values at both ends

stress.description: random array with pick targets always drawn from the array itself (guaranteed present)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 200)))
    nums = [rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    ops = ['Solution']
    args = [[nums]]
    calls = max(1, min(size, 50))
    for _ in range(calls):
        target = rng.choice(nums)
        ops.append('pick')
        args.append([target])
    return [ops, args]
